prevalence.value: significant contributor to acute viral hepatitis; outbreaks in waterborne settings
prevalence.trend: stable; outbreaks linked to water contamination and monsoon flooding

incidence.value: outbreak-dependent; common cause of acute hepatitis in adults

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated case-fatality in pregnancy (can exceed 10-20% in severe outbreaks)

riskFactors: immunosuppression (chronic infection risk with some genotypes)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Anti-HEV IgM and/or HEV RNA; evaluation in acute hepatitis syndromes, especially in outbreaks

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; outbreak-focused testing and hospital-based diagnosis
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: large acute hepatitis burden in outbreak years
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Supportive care; mortality driven by severe disease in pregnancy and acute liver failure cases

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Hospital-based serology is common; confirmatory PCR more available in tertiary centers/ICMR labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive management; intensive care for acute liver failure; public health control via water and sanitation interventions
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Waterborne outbreaks and high maternal mortality in severe cases, plus limited ICU capacity in some districts
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: ICMR and state outbreak response capacity and increasing access to diagnostics in tertiary centers
